scope: Read-only P6 retrospective review of product behavior, runtime proof, and owner-facing evidence.

findings.summary: Early P6 rounds accepted synthetic or generic parity proof before product-specific runtime behavior was demonstrated.
Desktop proof improved to packaged runtime evidence, but final owner handoff still needs clearer App identity and review bundle binding.
Product parity assertions still require owner-visible, real-state proof before a future milestone can claim acceptance.

recommendations: Use vertical product slices for the next milestone so product behavior, evidence, and review close together.
Require App identity, launch path, and visible review bundle binding before any owner-facing PASS.
Do not treat synthetic fixture success as real product parity.
